title: irlbachia alata subsp. angustifolia
intro: Irlbachia alata subsp. angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae, commonly known as a type of menispermaceous vine. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Irlbachia alata subsp. angustifolia may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although the specific preparation methods and dosages may vary depending on the traditional practice or region.
